$schema: Language-agnostic registration-file verification cases for fetchRegistrationFile / fetch_registration_file / fetch_registration_file_with. Each language's test iterates this table through its fetch/verify path with an injected fetcher (fetchImpl / fetch_impl / an injectable ByteFetcher seam). `contentBase64`, when present, is the exact byte content the injected fetcher must serve for `uri` (for schemes that hit the network: ipfs://, https://). `data:` URIs carry their content inline in `uri` itself and need no injection. `byteLength`, when present (oversize case), means: the injected fetcher must serve that many bytes (content immaterial) so the 2 MiB cap rejects it before any JSON parse is attempted. `expected.errorName` is the canonical error name (see api-manifest.json's `errorNames` list and its per-language casing/suffix rules); when set, the call is expected to fail and `verified`/`source`/`contentError` are not meaningful.
